articles.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
articles.snippet: Google has officially launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers significantly faster GPU and NPU acceleration alongside seamless support for PyTorch and JAX. The update also introduces lower-precision data type support for increased efficiency and a commitment to more frequent security and dependency updates across the TensorFlow ecosystem. This transition solidifies LiteRT as Google's primary high-performance framework for deploying GenAI and advanced on-device inference.
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that significantly improves GPU and NPU acceleration for AI applications. This update also enhances support for PyTorch and JAX, marking a shift in TensorFlow's deployment capabilities.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes AI deployment efficiency with a focus on high-performance frameworks.

articles.ai_summary: OpenSSL has released a security advisory detailing vulnerabilities in several versions, including moderate and low severity issues. Users are urged to update to mitigate risks associated with incorrect failure handling and other vulnerabilities.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights ongoing security challenges in widely used software libraries.

articles.title: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
articles.snippet:
GitHub Copilot CLI now lets you connect your own model provider or run fully local models instead of using GitHub-hosted model routing. This means you can use the models and…
The post Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
articles.ai_summary: GitHub Copilot CLI has introduced support for Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) and local models, allowing users to utilize their own model providers or run models locally. This flexibility enhances user control over AI model deployment.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers with greater control over AI model usage and security.

articles.title: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
articles.snippet:
Some dependency vulnerabilities require more than a version bump—they need code changes across your project. You can now assign Dependabot alerts to AI coding agents, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex,…
The post Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
articles.ai_summary: Dependabot now allows users to assign alerts for dependency vulnerabilities to AI agents like Copilot and Claude for remediation. This feature enables more efficient handling of complex code changes needed for vulnerabilities.
articles.why_it_matters: Streamlines vulnerability management by leveraging AI for code remediation.

articles.title: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
articles.snippet:
Dependabot now supports Nix flakes. Add nix as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml file. Dependabot will then monitor your flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when newer commits are…
The post Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
articles.ai_summary: Dependabot has expanded its capabilities to support the Nix ecosystem, allowing users to monitor Nix flakes for updates. This addition broadens the range of package ecosystems that Dependabot can manage.
articles.why_it_matters: Increases the versatility of Dependabot, catering to a wider developer audience.
